programmes.show_description: On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
Love Bites: Engaged couple Christopher and Tess inherited a New York diner, but working together has created a toxic work environment. Can Gordon help?
Air ambulances bring the emergency room to critically ill or injured people across the country, including a man in cardiac arrest and a woman who's been trampled by a foal.
In the centre of Brighton, a lone officer is violently attacked and urgently needs back-up. And a disorientated drunk going home to bed tries to smash his way into the wrong house.
Brighton's night police patrol the town's hectic night life - from a possible suicide up on the cliff, to an urgent manhunt for a violent assailant with a crowbar.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Opel Manta GTE: In Rochdale, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue former amateur rally driver Alan's Opel Manta GTE. Can they re-ignite Alan's spark?
Everything Must Go: On the trail of two vintage Harley-Davidsons in Pennsylvania, Frank gets more than he bargained for when one bike collector takes a liking to him!
In Bradford, Akili aims to enchant her guests with exotic elegance and a fusion of flavours. But will her ice cream leave her out in the cold? (Ep4/5)
The week's final visit is to Fritha and Justin's country hotel in Flintshire, where things get frosty over al fresco drinks, and breakfast is chaotic.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Tuscany, Gill and Robin turn an old cantina into a rural hillside retreat. Stacey sets her sights on opening a yoga studio. David and Lane's relocation doesn't go to plan.
It's full steam ahead for train driver Wayne and train manager Kate, as Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £85k budget can buy in Pinar de Campoverde in Spain.
Jane and James scour eastern Crete for an Aegean gem on a budget of £130k. Will a house packed full of charm or a villa with wrap-around views be the perfect place for them?
Liverpool. Young-at-heart barber Kenny Flynn, who's always in search of a laugh, hosts the first night with a 'Big Kids' party theme. (Ep1/5)
Liverpool. Business owner Rob Spicer dresses up as Margaret Thatcher, as part of his historical leaders theme, and is joined by guests including 'the Queen' and 'Naomi Campbell'. (Ep2/5)
Liverpool. Nigerian model and barrister Cynthia Umezulike hopes to raise the bar with her menu of fish, joloff rice and an exotic fruit platter. (Ep3/5)
Liverpool. Hairy rocker Glen Simpson serves sticky blackened pork and an Elvis-inspired dessert of deep-fried chocolate bars in peanut butter ice cream. (Ep4/5)
Liverpool. Jilly Pollock hosts the last night with a menu of Jilly's figs, Jilly's hot beef and Jilly's berry delight. Can some ballroom dancing heal the tension between her guests? (Ep5/5)
Tony and Hendrik hunt for their dream holiday home on Portugal's Setúbal Peninsula, for £330k. Can Danni Menzies find the perfect place for all their family to enjoy?
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Amy and Takie, who wanted rid of renting in London, and Vicky and Andy who hoped to combine a home with two businesses in Wiltshire.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Sue and Richard's beautiful home in Mapperley is no longer suitable since Richard had a cycling accident. Can Kirstie make it accessible? Or should they list it?
Less: Can Joe and Suzanne transform chaotic cooking into delightful dinners for the Burton family, including for two children with coeliac disease?
The Butcher of Suburbia: The landmark series brings us the gripping inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman - and the killer's macabre efforts to conceal her death. (Ep1/2)
The Butcher of Suburbia: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. (Ep2/2)
...the World: In March 2011, Japan was hit by a devastating tsunami, killing 20,000 and triggering a nuclear meltdown. Survivors and witnesses tell their extraordinary stories. (Ep1)
...the World: In March 2011, a massive tsunami caused a major meltdown at Japan's Fukushima nuclear plant, triggering explosions and evacuations. What went wrong at Fukushima? (Ep2)

programmes.show_name: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programmes.show_description: Ex-navy pilot Rich has a huge 16cm tumour running from his jaw to his heart. Meanwhile, nearly all the organs in Tracy's pelvis need removal to combat her stage four bowel cancer.
A man has broken his leg on Scafell Pike. As darkness closes in, can the mountain rescue team save him from potentially life-threatening plummeting temperatures? (Ep2/4)
On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
This time it's Corrie, The Stolen Girl, The Piano, Mickey Rourke on Celebrity Big Brother, savoury cocktails on Saturday Kitchen, the News on Katy Perry in space, and more.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Ford Escort RS2000: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend visit Dorset to fix up a Mk2 RS2000. It belongs to carpenter Mike, who gave up work to support his son who had a terrible accident.
KISS and Sell: Will expert buyers Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz want to rock and roll all night when they find masses of KISS merch in Nicky's barn? Plus: Mike goes for a test drive!
In Bradford, Tom draws on his French heritage to deliver simple dishes executed well. And as the final scores are revealed, who has that je ne sais quoi? (Ep5/5)
It's payment day following a wonderful week in Wales. The problem is, none of the B&Bers can agree on what exactly was wonderful about it.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Gill and Robin complete their Tuscan hillside retreat. Stretch and Rachael take on a busy summer season. And did David and Lane make the right move?
Geraldine and Tim are looking for a spacious holiday home that their whole family can enjoy on Spain's glorious Costa Tropical. Sara Damergi shows them what £180k can buy.
Lucy Alexander helps Tracy and Lee find a French holiday home in Lower Normandy, which they hope could one day become their permanent home, for their budget of £70k.
Crewe and Nantwich. Hairdresser Mushgon Dadfar serves up Caribbean cuisine. But how will her Zumba entertainment go down after a heavy meal? (Ep1/5)
Crewe and Nantwich. After years of practice as a host, Pip Massey has high expectations for her 'glamorous' evening. (Ep2/5)
Crewe and Nantwich. Landscape gardener and joker Darren's evening features fine food and lashings of his unique entertainment, including big laughs at Darren's bottom. (Ep3/5)
Crewe and Nantwich. Student Emma Davies serves up her inventive stuffed pork, home-made gnocchi and a musical performance. (Ep4/5)
Crewe and Nantwich. The week of dining is rounded off with hypnotherapist Russell Stubbs' 'British rock night'. Will Russell's great British produce win him the prize? (Ep5/5)
Maria and Andy love Cyprus. They met on the island 20 years ago, got married there, and are now planning to move there. Jean Johansson shows them what their £140k can buy in Famagusta.
...Ceremony - Signed and AD: Ade Adepitan, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey and Alice Tai present live from the opening ceremony of the Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics.
Andrew's sustained a severe facial injury in an accident at work. Tony's been crushed by a forklift truck. And in resus, 61-year-old John keeps having sudden blackouts. But why?
Brighton's night police patrol the town's hectic night life - from a possible suicide up on the cliff, to an urgent manhunt for a violent assailant with a crowbar.
The Goggleboxers settle down for this week's biggest and best TV.
